convention.forward: -Z (glTF). Unity's glTF importers flip Z, so these arrive facing +Z, which is the run direction.

models.name: CHR_Runner_Default
models.function: Default playable character. Replaces the cyan cube built in GameBootstrap.
models.pivot: Between the feet, y = 0, ground level
models.notes: The free starter. Deliberately the plainest silhouette in the set so every purchasable character is a visible upgrade.

models.name: CHR_Runner_Nova
models.function: Purchasable character 'char_nova' (2,500 coins).
models.pivot: Between the feet, y = 0, ground level
models.notes: Aero crest and calf fins mark it as the speed-read character.

models.name: CHR_Runner_Nova_Void
models.function: Skin 'skin_nova_void' (350 gems). Nova's geometry, Void materials.
models.pivot: Between the feet, y = 0, ground level
models.notes: Shares Nova's mesh exactly — a skin must never change the silhouette, or players read it as a hitbox change. Only the material set differs, so at runtime this can be a material swap on the Nova mesh rather than a second mesh in memory.

models.name: CHR_Runner_Rook
models.function: Purchasable character 'char_rook' (7,500 coins).
models.pivot: Between the feet, y = 0, ground level
models.notes: Armoured and blocky — the prestige tier. Heavier build, identical collision footprint: cosmetics never touch balance (StoreItem.cs).

models.name: OBS_LowJump_Barrier
models.function: ObstacleKind.LowJump — a grounded block cleared only by jumping.
models.pivot: Box centre (0,0,0). Place at ObstacleArchetype.CentreY = 0.35 m.
models.notes: Chevrons point up because the required input is up. The player has ~250 ms to read this at 30 m/s; an arrow is faster to parse than a colour convention they have to learn.

models.name: OBS_HighSlide_Gate
models.function: ObstacleKind.HighSlide — an overhead barrier cleared only by sliding.
models.pivot: Box centre (0,0,0). Place at ObstacleArchetype.CentreY = 1.6 m.
models.notes: Nothing in this mesh drops below the box's own bottom edge. That is the hard constraint: the gap beneath is the entire mechanic, and a decorative strut hanging 5 cm too low would kill a correct slide.

models.name: OBS_FullBlock_Wall
models.function: ObstacleKind.FullBlock — a full-height wall; the only answer is a lane change.
models.pivot: Box centre (0,0,0). Place at ObstacleArchetype.CentreY = 0.8 m.
models.notes: Reads as unambiguously solid: a filled grid rather than the open framing of the other two. The lateral arrows tell the player the answer is sideways, since there is no vertical solution to find.

models.name: PU_Coin
models.function: The run currency. Replaces PrimitiveFactory.Coin (radius 0.35 m).
models.pivot: Disc centre. Place at TrackStreamer.CoinHeight = 0.9 m.
models.notes: Disc axis runs along Z so the face is square to the camera, matching the 90-degrees-on-X rotation PrimitiveFactory.Coin already applies. The bevelled rim keeps a lit sliver visible while it bobs; a flat cylinder vanishes edge-on and the coin appears to flicker out.

models.name: PU_Magnet
models.function: PowerUpType.Magnet — pulls nearby coins in for a few seconds.
models.pivot: Bounding-box centre. Place at TrackStreamer.PowerUpHeight = 1.0 m.
models.notes: A literal horseshoe magnet with lit pole tips. Silhouette over colour: the three pickups are told apart while tumbling, when colour is washing out into the bloom and only the outline survives.

models.name: PU_Shield
models.function: PowerUpType.Shield — absorbs the next hit. A charge, not a timer.
models.pivot: Bounding-box centre. Place at TrackStreamer.PowerUpHeight = 1.0 m.
models.notes: Faceted crystal core inside an open hex ring. The ring is the visual promise of a barrier; the translucent core is the one place in the project a blended material earns its overdraw, because the pickup is on screen for under a second and never in bulk.

models.name: PU_DoubleScore
models.function: PowerUpType.DoubleScore — multiplies distance score for a few seconds.
models.pivot: Bounding-box centre. Place at TrackStreamer.PowerUpHeight = 1.0 m.
models.notes: A raised x2 glyph on a gold tablet. Numerals beat abstraction here: the multiplier is the only pickup whose value is a quantity, and the player should not have to remember which colour meant how much.

models.name: TRK_RoadSlab_30m
models.function: One 30 m road chunk. Replaces the road cube built in TrackStreamer.BuildChunkShell.
models.pivot: Slab centre. Matches localPosition (0, -0.1, ChunkLength*0.5).
models.notes: Modelled at exactly one ChunkLength so chunks butt together with no seam and no scaling. The UVs tile once per 3 m, which is short enough that the panel seams give a speed cue and long enough that they do not strobe at 30 m/s.

models.name: TRK_LaneMarker_30m
models.function: One lane divider. Replaces the lane marker cubes in TrackStreamer.
models.pivot: Strip centre. Place at x = +/- LaneWidth*0.5, y = 0.005.
models.notes: Kept as a continuous strip rather than dashes: dashes at 30 m/s alias into a strobe on a 60 Hz panel, and the marker's job is to be a stable reference the eye can track, not decoration.

models.name: TRK_EdgeRail_30m
models.function: Neon guard rail along both road edges. New — no greybox equivalent.
models.pivot: Rail centre at its base. Place at x = +/- ROAD_WIDTH/2, y = 0.
models.notes: Inferred addition. The greybox road just stops at its edge and the eye has nothing to measure lateral speed against; a lit rail in the periphery is the cheapest possible speed cue and costs 2 draw calls per chunk.

models.name: ENV_Building_Low
models.function: Short skyline block. Replaces the low end of TrackStreamer's random building range.
models.pivot: Base centre, y = 0 at ground level
models.notes: Three fixed heights replace the greybox's continuous random height. A fixed set can share one mesh and one material, so the whole skyline GPU-instances; a continuously random scale cannot batch and would put dozens of draw calls into scenery nobody looks at.

models.name: ENV_Building_Mid
models.function: Mid skyline block.
models.pivot: Base centre, y = 0 at ground level
models.notes: Mid tier. Vary the skyline by mixing the three prefabs and rotating them 90 degrees, not by scaling — non-uniform scale would stretch the facade texture and break the crown's proportions.

models.name: ENV_Building_Tall
models.function: Tall skyline block; the one that reads above the fog line.
models.pivot: Base centre, y = 0 at ground level
models.notes: Tall enough to break the fog end distance, which is what gives the horizon depth instead of a flat wall of haze.

models.name: ENV_NeonStrip_Crown
models.function: Standalone rooftop light band, for dressing buildings not in the set.
models.pivot: Strip centre. Place at building top + half its own height.
models.notes: Extracted so the crown can be reused on any block; matches the greybox strip in TrackStreamer (body width + 0.15, 0.15 tall).

models.name: ENV_Billboard_Sign
models.function: Roadside neon billboard. New — inferred set dressing.
models.pivot: Base of the post, y = 0.
models.notes: Inferred. The skyline is all vertical blocks, so it reads as a repeating comb; a billboard is a horizontal element that breaks that rhythm. Frame is lit, panel is dark, so it silhouettes against the fog rather than adding another bloom source competing with the coins.

models.name: ENV_ArchGate
models.function: Neon arch spanning the full road width. New — inferred milestone marker.
models.pivot: Centre of the road at ground level, y = 0.
models.notes: Inferred, with a gameplay job: StageService advances the run through named stages but nothing in the world marks the transition. An arch the player runs through is a free, diegetic stage boundary. Its clear height is 5.2 m — far above the 2.1 m ceiling of any obstacle — so it can never be mistaken for something that must be dodged.

models.name: STR_Board_Pulse
models.function: Store item 'board_pulse' (4,000 coins).
models.pivot: Deck centre, y = 0 at the deck's mid-plane
models.notes: Boards are pure cosmetics with no gameplay effect, so they are modelled as a display prop first: readable from the store's three-quarter angle, with the lit surfaces on the underside where a turntable shows them off.

models.name: STR_Board_Prism
models.function: Store item 'board_prism' (500 gems).
models.pivot: Deck centre, y = 0 at the deck's mid-plane
models.notes: The premium tier gets a refractive nose crystal — a silhouette change, not just a recolour, because a gem-priced item that differs only in hue reads as a bad deal next to a coin-priced one.

models.name: STR_Gem
models.function: The premium currency (CurrencyType.Gems). Used in the store, HUD and reward popups.
models.pivot: Gem centre
models.notes: A cut octahedron with a crown of facets: eight tris for the core plus the girdle. At HUD size this is fewer triangles than the quad a sprite would need, and it can spin, which a sprite cannot.

models.name: STR_CoinStack
models.function: Soft-currency icon for CurrencyPack items (gems_100 ... gems_5000) and reward popups.
models.pivot: Base of the stack, y = 0
models.notes: Four coins, not one: a pack is a quantity and the stack says so without a number. Reuses PU_Coin's exact profile so the shop icon and the thing picked up in a run are visibly the same object.

models.name: STR_Chest_Bundle
models.function: Bundle icon for 'bundle_starter' and the StarterPackOffer screen.
models.pivot: Base centre, y = 0
models.notes: A chest with the lid cracked and light escaping. The offer screen has one job — communicate 'more than one thing inside' — and an open lid does it in the 300 ms before the player reads any text.

models.name: STR_Crown_Vip
models.function: Icon for the VIP subscription ('vip_monthly') and the VipScreen.
models.pivot: Base centre, y = 0
models.notes: Crown rather than a badge: subscription is status, and the item sits next to consumables in the same list where it needs to look categorically different at a glance.

models.name: STR_Badge_NoAds
models.function: Icon for the 'no_ads' IAP (ItemKind.AdRemoval).
models.pivot: Badge centre; disc axis along Z, square to the camera
models.notes: A struck disc with a bar across it. Reads as a prohibition sign at icon size, where a literal 'no advertisement' illustration would not.
